evals.prompt: I want to build a simple CNN classifier in Rust using Burn for the CIFAR-10 dataset. Write a complete setup: Cargo.toml, model.rs with a conv+linear stack, and a main.rs that initializes the model on WGPU. Use the current Burn 0.21 API — I don't want the old B: Backend generic style.
evals.expected_output: A working Burn 0.21 project with Cargo.toml (burn dependency with wgpu+train+vision features), model struct without B: Backend generics using #[derive(Module)], Device::wgpu() selection, and main.rs with #![recursion_limit = "256"].
expectations: Cargo.toml includes burn with features wgpu, train, vision
Model struct uses #[derive(Module, Debug)] without B: Backend generic
No Tensor<B, D> style — uses Tensor<D> without backend generic

evals.expected_output: A complete migration guide covering: remove B: Backend generic from struct/impl, replace Tensor<B,D> with Tensor<D>, Ignored<T> → #[module(skip)], PaddingConfig1d::Explicit(1) → Explicit(1,1), Gelu → Gelu::new(), DType::Bool → DType::Bool(_) or DType::Bool(BoolStore::Native), and mention that BinFileRecorder records are not forward-compatible.
expectations: Explains removing B: Backend generic from struct and impl
Covers DType::Bool → DType::Bool(_) match arm update
Mentions BinFileRecorder forward-compat or record migration warning

evals.prompt: I need to run Burn inference in a WebAssembly browser app (no GPU, pure CPU). Which backend should I use? Also, tell me what Cargo.toml features I need and how to load model weights from a compiled-in bytes slice so I don't hit the filesystem at runtime. My model is already trained and saved as a .bpk file.
evals.expected_output: Recommend burn-flex (the new 0.21 pure-Rust CPU backend replacing burn-ndarray for WASM). Cargo features: flex + store, default-features = false. Load weights via ModuleRecord::from_bytes(include_bytes!("model.bpk")). Mention burn-ndarray is deprecated.
expectations: Cargo features: flex, store, default-features = false
Uses include_bytes! to embed model weights at compile time
